# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(
            name: 'IMAGE_TAG',
            defaultValue: '',
            description: 'Docker image tag (e.g., v1.2.3, latest). Only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, and underscores allowed.'
        )
        choice(
            name: 'REGISTRY_URL',
            choices: [
                'registry.example.com',
                'staging-registry.example.com',
                'prod-registry.example.com'
            ],
            description: 'Target Docker registry URL (select from approved registries)'
        )
        string(
            name: 'IMAGE_NAME',
            defaultValue: '',
            description: 'Docker image name (e.g., myapp/backend). Only alphanumeric, slashes, dots, hyphens, and underscores allowed.'
        )
    }

    environment {
        REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS_ID = 'docker-registry-credentials'
        DOCKERFILE_PATH = './Dockerfile'
        BUILD_CONTEXT = '.'
    }

    stages {
        stage('Validate Parameters') {
            steps {
                script {
                    // Validate IMAGE_TAG: only allow alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores
                    if (!params.IMAGE_TAG || params.IMAGE_TAG.trim().isEmpty()) {
                        error('IMAGE_TAG parameter is required and cannot be empty.')
                    }
                    if (!(params.IMAGE_TAG ==~ /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]{0,127}$/)) {
                        error("IMAGE_TAG '${params.IMAGE_TAG}' contains invalid characters. Only alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, and underscores are allowed (max 128 chars, must start with alphanumeric).")
                    }

                    // Validate IMAGE_NAME: only allow alphanumeric, slashes, dots, hyphens, underscores
                    if (!params.IMAGE_NAME || params.IMAGE_NAME.trim().isEmpty()) {
                        error('IMAGE_NAME parameter is required and cannot be empty.')
                    }
                    if (!(params.IMAGE_NAME ==~ /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._\/-]{0,255}$/)) {
                        error("IMAGE_NAME '${params.IMAGE_NAME}' contains invalid characters. Only alphanumeric, slashes, dots, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.")
                    }
                    // Prevent path traversal in IMAGE_NAME
                    if (params.IMAGE_NAME.contains('..')) {
                        error("IMAGE_NAME must not contain '..' sequences.")
                    }

                    // Validate REGISTRY_URL against allowlist (choice parameter already constrains this,
                    // but we double-check in case of API-triggered builds)
                    def allowedRegistries = [
                        'registry.example.com',
                        'staging-registry.example.com',
                        'prod-registry.example.com'
                    ]
                    if (!allowedRegistries.contains(params.REGISTRY_URL)) {
                        error("REGISTRY_URL '${params.REGISTRY_URL}' is not in the approved registry allowlist: ${allowedRegistries}")
                    }

                    // Store validated values in environment variables for safe downstream use
                    env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_TAG = params.IMAGE_TAG.trim()
                    env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_NAME = params.IMAGE_NAME.trim()
                    env.VALIDATED_REGISTRY_URL = params.REGISTRY_URL.trim()
                    env.FULL_IMAGE_REF = "${env.VALIDATED_REGISTRY_URL}/${env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_NAME}:${env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_TAG}"

                    echo "Validated build parameters:"
                    echo "  Registry: ${env.VALIDATED_REGISTRY_URL}"
                    echo "  Image: ${env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_NAME}"
                    echo "  Tag: ${env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_TAG}"
                    echo "  Full reference: ${env.FULL_IMAGE_REF}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                checkout scm
            }
        }

        stage('Verify Dockerfile') {
            steps {
                script {
                    if (!fileExists(env.DOCKERFILE_PATH)) {
                        error("Dockerfile not found at path: ${env.DOCKERFILE_PATH}")
                    }
                    echo "Dockerfile found at ${env.DOCKERFILE_PATH}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Build Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    // Use Docker Pipeline plugin's API for safe image building
                    // This avoids shell injection by not interpolating user input into sh commands
                    docker.build(
                        env.FULL_IMAGE_REF,
                        "--no-cache --pull -f ${env.DOCKERFILE_PATH} ${env.BUILD_CONTEXT}"
                    )
                    echo "Successfully built image: ${env.FULL_IMAGE_REF}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Scan Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    // Run a basic security scan before pushing
                    // Using the validated full image reference via Docker plugin API
                    echo "Running security scan on ${env.FULL_IMAGE_REF}"
                    def img = docker.image(env.FULL_IMAGE_REF)
                    // If Trivy or another scanner is available, invoke it safely
                    // Using withEnv to pass values without shell interpolation
                    withEnv(["SCAN_TARGET=${env.FULL_IMAGE_REF}"]) {
                        sh '''
                            if command -v trivy >/dev/null 2>&1; then
                                trivy image --exit-code 1 --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --no-progress "$SCAN_TARGET"
                            else
                                echo "WARNING: Trivy not found, skipping vulnerability scan"
                            fi
                        '''
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Push Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    // Use Jenkins credentials plugin to securely handle registry authentication
                    // Credentials are stored in Jenkins credential store, never in plaintext
                    docker.withRegistry("https://${env.VALIDATED_REGISTRY_URL}", env.REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS_ID) {
                        def img = docker.image(env.FULL_IMAGE_REF)
                        img.push()
                        echo "Successfully pushed image: ${env.FULL_IMAGE_REF}"

                        // Also push with build number tag for traceability
                        def buildTag = "${env.VALIDATED_REGISTRY_URL}/${env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_NAME}:build-${env.BUILD_NUMBER}"
                        img.push("build-${env.BUILD_NUMBER}")
                        echo "Successfully pushed image with build tag: build-${env.BUILD_NUMBER}"
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Cleanup') {
            steps {
                script {
                    // Remove local images to free disk space
                    // Using withEnv to safely pass the image reference without shell interpolation
                    withEnv(["CLEANUP_IMAGE=${env.FULL_IMAGE_REF}", "CLEANUP_BUILD_IMAGE=${env.VALIDATED_REGISTRY_URL}/${env.VALIDATED_IMAGE_NAME}:build-${env.BUILD_NUMBER}"]) {
                        sh '''
                            docker rmi "$CLEANUP_IMAGE" || true
                            docker rmi "$CLEANUP_BUILD_IMAGE" || true
                        '''
                    }
                    echo "Local image cleanup completed"
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        success {
            echo "Pipeline completed successfully. Image ${env.FULL_IMAGE_REF} has been built and pushed."
        }
        failure {
            echo "Pipeline failed. Please check the logs for details."
        }
        always {
            // Clean workspace to prevent sensitive data leakage
            cleanWs()
        }
    }
}
